description: Fix resource code examples in the Resources & Prompts guide to use the uri parameter in read() return values instead of hardcoding the URI string. The current examples define read(uri: string) but then use string literals in the return, causing @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars lint errors when a plugin developer copies them verbatim.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Use uri parameter in resource examples in resources-prompts.mdx
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the resource code examples in the docs to use the uri parameter in the read() return value so that copying them into my plugin doesn't cause lint errors.
acceptanceCriteria: In the 'Defining a Resource' code example (~line 35-47), the read() function uses the uri parameter in the return object instead of the hardcoded string 'slack://channels'
In the 'Typed Resources with Schema' code example (~line 127-143), the read() function uses the uri parameter in the return object instead of the hardcoded string 'myapp://channels'
In the 'Full Plugin Example' TrackerPlugin code (~line 435-446), the itemList resource's read() function uses the uri parameter in the return object instead of the hardcoded string 'tracker://items'
All three examples still show the correct uri value in the defineResource config (the uri property on the resource definition itself remains a string literal)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
The file to edit is docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx.

There are three resource examples that all have the same issue — the read(uri) function hardcodes the URI string in the return value instead of using the uri parameter.

Example 1 ('Defining a Resource', around line 35-47):
Change `return { uri: 'slack://channels', text: ... }` to `return { uri, text: ... }`

Example 2 ('Typed Resources with Schema', around line 127-143):
Change `return { uri: 'myapp://channels', text: ... }` to `return { uri, text: ... }` (both the success and fallback paths)

Example 3 ('Full Plugin Example' TrackerPlugin, around line 435-446):
Change `return { uri: 'tracker://items', text: ... }` to `return { uri, text: ... }` (both the success and fallback paths)

Do NOT change the uri property on the defineResource config itself — that's the resource's identity and should remain a string literal. Only change the return values inside the read() function body.

The fix is straightforward: replace the hardcoded URI string with the `uri` parameter in each return statement. Use shorthand property syntax: `{ uri, text: ... }` instead of `{ uri: 'tracker://items', text: ... }`.
